advantages: Domain-informed columns often lift model performance with minimal compute—no extra training data required.
Explicit formulas are interpretable: stakeholders can audit price_per_sqft or days_since_purchase unlike opaque embeddings.
Created features compose with any estimator and run cheaply in SQL, pandas, or streaming jobs at serve time.
Targeted interactions fix linear model blind spots without exploding tree depth or neural parameter count.
disadvantages: Poorly chosen features add noise and multicollinearity—many ratios may duplicate information already in raw columns.
Aggregate and rolling features leak labels or future data if cutoff windows are wrong, silently inflating offline metrics.
Manual feature catalogs require maintenance when business rules, units, or schemas change across deployments.
Exhaustive cross-product interactions blow up dimensionality and overfit small datasets unless regularized or pruned.
